summary: Regression testing completed for deployment hardening changes: backend env loading, lazy Mongo startup behavior, frontend proxy target usage, preview root availability, and full backend suite stability.

ui_bugs.issue: Password input and Authenticate button do not expose data-testid attributes, reducing selector stability for future UI automation.

issues: Footer helper text remains low-contrast on dark background.

action_items: Optional quality: add data-testid attributes on login controls.
Optional quality: improve login footer text contrast.
Optional hardening: decouple app/state.py from implicit env preloading (standalone import currently raises KeyError if env was not loaded first).
critical_code_review_comments: Deployment-focused hardening is effective: app.main loads backend/.env first, and state connection is lazy (no DB connect on import).
frontend/server.js correctly requires BACKEND_PROXY_TARGET and fails fast if BACKEND_PROXY_TARGET/HOST/PORT are missing.
Code coupling remains: importing app.state directly before app.main currently fails due to required env vars at module import time.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Executed full pytest at /app/tests with JUnit output and validated preview URL root/login/dashboard flow via browser automation. Verified app-main import remains successful even with invalid MONGO_URL and state client remains uninitialized until first DB access.
rca of the issue: No blocking deployment regression reproduced. Preview routing works, backend APIs respond, and suite is green. Remaining items are low-priority testability/accessibility quality gaps.
